commentary_text: The model assigns Croatia a 79% win probability while the sportsbook consensus sits at 58%, a 21-percentage-point gap favoring the model's confidence in a Croatian victory. The market also prices Ghana's chances at 17% compared to the model's 3%, suggesting real-money flows are hedging against an upset scenario that the model's Elo-based framework views as unlikely. This divergence may reflect recent lineup changes, injury news, or current form data available to traders but outside the model's knowledge—information that could materially shift win probability for either side. The dashboard flags this as a signal worthy of attention rather than a judgment on which source is more accurate.
